back_translation: On the fifteenth day of the second month after they had left the land of Egypt, the whole congregation of the sons of Israel set out from Elim and came to the Wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai.
In that wilderness, the whole congregation of the sons of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron.

The sons of Israel said to them, 'If only we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt! There we sat by pots of meat and ate bread until full, but you have brought us into this wilderness to make this whole assembly die of hunger.'

The LORD said to Moses, 'Behold, I will rain down bread from heaven for you. Each day the people will go out and gather enough for that day, so that I may test them whether they will walk according to my law or not.'
'On the sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it will be twice as much as they gather on other days.'
Moses and Aaron said to all the sons of Israel, 'This evening you will know that the LORD has brought you out of the land of Egypt,'
'and in the morning you will see the glory of the LORD, because he has heard your grumbling against him. Who are we, that you should grumble against us?'

Moses added, 'The LORD will give you meat to eat in the evening and bread to fill you in the morning, because he has heard your grumbling which you grumble against him. Who are we? Your grumbling is not against us, but against the LORD.'
Moses said to Aaron, 'Tell the whole congregation of the sons of Israel: Come before the LORD, for he has heard your grumbling.'
As Aaron was speaking to the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, they turned toward the wilderness, and behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.
The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'I have heard the grumbling of the sons of Israel. Tell them: At twilight you will eat meat, and in the morning you will be filled with bread. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God.'
That evening, quail came up and covered the camp, and in the morning there was a layer of dew around the camp.
When the layer of dew evaporated, behold, on the surface of the wilderness there were thin flakes, fine like hoarfrost on the ground.
When the sons of Israel saw it, they said to one another, 'What is this?' For they did not know what it was. Moses said to them, 'This is the bread that the LORD has given you to eat.'

'This is what the LORD has commanded: Gather of it, each one according to what he can eat — one omer per person, according to the number of people in your tents; each of you take for those in his tent.'
The sons of Israel did so. Some gathered much, some gathered little.
When they measured it by the omer, he who gathered much had no excess, and he who gathered little had no lack. Each one gathered as much as he needed to eat.
Moses said to them, 'Do not let anyone keep manna until morning.'
But they did not listen to Moses; some kept part of the manna until morning, and it bred worms and stank. Moses was angry with them.
Every morning each one gathered manna as much as he needed; when the sun grew hot, the manna melted.
On the sixth day, they gathered twice as much food — two omers per person — and all the leaders of the congregation came and reported this to Moses.

He told them, 'This is what the LORD has said: Tomorrow is a day of complete rest, a holy Sabbath to the LORD. Bake what you will bake, and boil what you will boil. The rest, set aside for yourselves to keep until morning.'
They set the manna aside until morning as Moses had commanded, and it did not stink and had no worms in it.
Moses said, 'Eat the manna today, for today is a Sabbath to the LORD; today you will not find manna in the field.'
'For six days you will gather, but on the seventh day, the Sabbath, there will be none.'
But on the seventh day, some of the people went out to gather, and they did not find any.
The LORD said to Moses, 'How long will you refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?'
'Understand that the LORD has given you the Sabbath; therefore on the sixth day he gives you bread for two days. Let everyone stay in his place; do not let anyone leave his place on the seventh day.'
So the people rested on the seventh day.
The house of Israel called the bread's name manna. It was white like coriander seed, and its taste was like wafers made with honey.

Moses said, 'This is what the LORD has commanded: Keep a full omer of manna for all your generations, so that they may see the bread I fed you in the wilderness when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.'
Moses said to Aaron, 'Take a jar, fill it with a full omer of manna, and place it before the LORD to be kept for all your generations.'
As the LORD had commanded Moses, Aaron placed it before the Testimony to be preserved.
The sons of Israel ate manna forty years, until they came to a land that could be settled; they ate manna until they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
(An omer is one-tenth of an ephah.)
